A Fee presentation mistakes, which we are on page 37 You're following along in the songbook Number one is of course establishing the precedent of free It's hard to take that back Very difficult so when you when you do free you got to think long and hard about it J. Abraham has a wrap about Which is the way he handles it. He has a whole rift worth hearing about That he has decided For reasons x y and z to buy you a day of his time And so making an exception and actually buying you his day Really he's doing a free day Now I don't like it under any circumstances, but it's a better way to say it It ought to be the exception when there is free Writing to assignment we talked about that talking about money too soon So that's right in line with writing to a sign that's the The answering the fee question or disclosing the fee before there has been a diagnostic conversation So you're now talking money too soon So the How much do you charge to do a full-page magazine ad question will never get an answer for me? Because it's the wrong question a I don't know if we're going to do a full-page magazine and B. I don't know if we're going to do one or we're going to do 20 variant And I don't know what else we're going to do. I do projects not pieces So let's have the conversation about What the objective is and how we're going to get to the objective then let's talk about the money? But we don't want to be talking about money first Switching to a selling mode You have to sell without it being overtly Seen and perceived and felt as selling Which again is why diagnosis has to be part of the process The the selling mode is also so the question that you just asked right Selling salespeople typically are defensive against objections So the worst salespeople in the world play ping-pong with their prospect So the prospect raises an objection they vigorously answer the objection Prospect raises another objection. They vigorously answer the objection People who are really good at it can sell that way and make a living But it's kind of an ugly process and it is clearly selling So the position you don't want to be in is vigorously defending yourself against objection After objection after objection you're perceived now as selling not his consulting and we want to be perceived as consulting So for the most part you solve it by getting all that stuff out ahead of time And you being the one to raise it not them raising it inviting negotiation How do we invite negotiation well attitudinally people do it Conversationally it comes up I have occasionally Recently had somebody you know Well, do you negotiate fee? No You know is this negotiable? Oh? You know and there's a process for figuring out what the fees gonna be which they see the process implemented So I'll show you that a little later. I wanted to share this I did on money too soon. I just got this This is April 16th It's somebody that sent in I Asked my newsletter subscribers for stuff because I'm working on a book about price strategies And this is they sent this in this is B2B marketer. I'm just gonna I'm not gonna read you the whole letter But there it is, but so there's a company called wheat mark. I'm not sure I even know what they do. Maybe he tells us Finally by May 1st, we were able to do it I think it's the single most important price strategy that you teach we took our prices off our website and stopped listing them all together The move was a scary departure for us, but soon we were laughing all the way to the bank as we doubled sales month to month from April to May Here's just a few of the advantages we found of not having prices listed anywhere number one It encourages a consultative approach to selling rather than an order-taking approach number two It greatly reduces apples to apples price comparisons number three. It strengthens positioning in the prospect of smiling Apprentices we make them apply to talk to us, too And number four it allows us to raise prices at will but the single most important reason to take your prices off your website Is that doing so allows you to choose your customers? and He's absolutely right. I mean Priceless are for pizza shops Priceless are not for professionals You don't walk into the Cleveland Clinic and find a price list there may be a day but Here's how you know if you have obama care If the if the directions to your doctor's office say Turn left at the trailer park Actually have whole roster of them, but we don't have But but you don't go into the Cleveland Clinic and find a price list That's not the way it works. And so it shouldn't work that way for us either Problem clients problems with clients very quickly. So one is a client that just doesn't get it They don't understand what it is that we do and they're going to require way too much time in educating them Defending everything that you're doing you have to make your decision about whether or not you want to be in that role or not Number two refusal or inability to work with you as agree key words. There are as agree This whole agreements process with the client and again, I'm going to give you contracts. I'm going to give you correspondence We're going to get there This whole agreements process with the client of here's how things are going to work It's very important to have it so you can refer to it when they start to deviate See trying to fix them has bad behavior surface that weren't discussed to begin with It can't be done. It's not even fair So you want an agreements process if you're not familiar with it from the personal development side It's an old book, but I suggest you find it. It's called the book of est And it is and it's an inside account of Going through the S program you laugh by the way, why do you laugh? Which oh well, yeah, but that's its point Um Werner and I are getting our hair cut next to each other and that's for a hair salon in San Francisco first time I met and I said I Knew his predecessors So we're very hard men it asked you know and so I said Off the record Some up some up your business asked in 20 words or less He says we preach independence and we breathe They mastered sell it by zealot, but maybe better than anybody else, but anyway you should get the book of est It's irrelevant what you think of est But They probably had the best agreements process really the first three hours of the seminar was the agreements process And it's in there almost work for So it's worth reading and seeing a good agreement process The agreements process with the client is important and it needs to be paper-trailed not just said so of course Bond it's a contract you'll see it because then when they start to deviate you get to play fair you can say wait a minute Here's what we agreed to All right Interference by committee Which has to be dealt with in your agreements process because this is one of the most common problems Is you thought you were dealing with John and now all of a sudden John's got a whole closet full of Netflix Who are weighing in on the process? It might be his board of directors It might be his mother-in-law it might be who knows but all of a sudden you're not dealing with John you're dealing with some Committee and you and maybe you can't even get to the committee. Well, that's no good So that has to be controlled from the beginning Personality conflicts conflicts of interest of course are legitimate if you take work outside your ability to perform If you really are in over your head That's a problem dealing with clients of questionable integrity in their relationships with you and people like you Not necessarily questionable integrity in general that would rule out 30% of everybody I've ever worked for So I'm not running a church. I mean so But there has to be honor among thieves if you will and so Like if they arrive at my doorstep now Cold I kind of know they've got problems somewhere else Because I'm the most expensive guy on the planet. So the reason they're there Right is because four other people are fired so my deal is I need to have litmus tests and one is you know If I can't find three people who've done business with you who say they would do business with you again We're not going to do business Because I kind of know I'm not going to be the one to cure all the bad behavior clients with actual or probable legal problems You'll see in my contracts language about all this but you make your own decisions because you can Wind up sitting in a room with the Attorney General of Iowa having a discussion at some length about your work with client a so You make your own decisions about that, but you ought to be aware of cognizant of it careful of it Clients who operate under FTC consent decrees. I have three of them right now. It's okay as long as I know But in some respects, it's actually better because they're constrained From doing certain things they would normally like to do But if you don't know that's a whole nother issue altogether Emergencies clients with emergencies you can get a lot of money from them But you have very low likelihood of success and very low likelihood of long-term relationships, so Full-verse story this morning. I'm not going to take time to tell it now But the 911 call because they are in trouble for one reason or another You really you have to make conscious choices about all that it usually requires you to drop everything else you're doing in order to perform well and And you almost know going in the result won't be good The result will just be not as bad as it would be So it's the stimulus argument, right? We can't prove it did anything But imagine how much worse everything would be if we hadn't done it. That's the position you'll be in and so You better be well paid for What are you doing someone's retreating from a deal we'll get to that correspondence I'm going to skip the entire Just gonna skip a bunch of stuff jump to page 44 This is important So there's what I call in every business and in every task there's a there's a good enough spot so So first of all not all G spots are in the same place This is extremely frustrating to males One or two, huh? Okay Well, they're not okay, so forget copy think about a simple business So Southwest Airlines has a different GE spot than than America You have certain expectations of Southwest about which you will controls your satisfaction And you're okay would be an hurt it on like cattle And the stampede at the gate and the little colored passes That's the They only give you peanuts and nothing else and you don't care about it because you could set up in advance Southwest is all about getting where you're going on time. That's the deal. That's their G spot and almost nothing else matters You fly first class on American You have a entirely different set of expectations therefore they're stuck with an entirely different G spot So all businesses have it, right? If we were in a rats You would it get rich Carlton is a different G spot than this hotel in terms of what's in the room the personnel Everything right so it's situational. It's never absolute the most important thing is There is little benefit to you in achieving more than good enough results Unless Your royalties are tied in to maximum results Otherwise and even then sometimes So if you spend too much time achieving Let's just use some simple so a Cost per sale is 200 bucks and you get it down to 180 If you spend a ton of time getting it down to 165 But and now don't move the client on to six other opportunities That were bigger and better opportunities in his business and therefore better opportunities for you to work on and he was Happy with 180 Right, you've accomplished nothing with all that effort to get it down to 165 They're not even He's making plenty of money at 165. He's happy at 165. He thinks you're a genius at 165 Move on to the next thing especially if the next thing is an even bigger better opportunity so There may be a chair further away from the furnace in the afterlife For maximum results in every situation. I don't know but there's no real reward here Unless it is directly affecting your money and Even then you have to be careful not to be getting too much over here at the expense of Not also going over here and going over here and going over here and going over here So right now I have a client They came to me for front end. They want to improve their front end results first transaction conversion of custom The big opportunity is in they have a 35 percent refund Okay, now they're making a ton of money, but so they like are ignoring it The big opportunity is there huge Because they're not doing anything to control So with a series of stick letters sales letters that remind you why you bought it first place and make you happy about buying it Right, I'm probably cut that to 20 percent. There's 15 points there And it's a ton of money So all I'm going to do with the front end is enough to improve where they're at. I Want to be done with that a week because I want to spend my time over here Now if I spent four months And I did more research and I did more work and I did more variables and we tested more variables Could I improve the front end even more than I'm going to yes? And maybe if we have a long enough relationship, I'll get all the way back around But right now I want to do just enough to satisfy them on that count so we can focus on this Where there's an enormous amount of money to be had and Guess what on the front end because the cost of acquisition is very high anyway The kind of business they're in the size of volume they do they can only give me a 1% Royalty on this that they don't that they don't think is any big opportunity anyway We're splitting the reduction in the refunds Well If I'm right about 15 points seven points of that's my Now eventually they're gonna want to buy it out which I'll show you in a contract which is fine, but say For them and for me. It's better to go spend the time over here than it is to spend the time over here So good enough good enough, right? Okay, I want to start showing you some things So on page 46 You have a page looks like this For the sake of speed because you haven't in your book. I'm not going to put them up on this thing Okay, you can't see them up there anyway You're gonna ride along with me in the book and I'm gonna look at my mind. Okay. What everybody good Okay, so page 46 this is The basic language if we're gonna have a consulting Relationship now you can read all this at home. I'm just gonna call attention to a couple things if you want to mark them you can So number one essentially says no no warranty of non disclosure So item a is no warranty of non disclosure or confidentiality That's important because Signing non disclosure agreements and signing confidentiality agreements is an invitation to be litigated against That's what it is And I don't know about you, but I haven't seen anybody yet with an original idea They all think they got but they don't I Got 50 duplicates in my file drawer And too much stuff crosses my desk this comes up a lot by the way for me And the answer always is I don't sign them. I don't agree to them. Here's why I don't Furthermore, here's the paper trail that not only didn't I sign yours you sign mine that says No agreement and I'm disclosure or confidentiality number two indemnity The indemnity clause essentially says whatever the hell they do with what I give them. It's on them It's not on me and if anybody asks me So if I do have to have that conversation with the Attorney General in Iowa or the head of consumer protection in Tupelo or Whatever they're paying all the costs for it. It's on them Three is maybe the most important thing here This is it's it's headlined the word access the real that word here is limitation Right, so this spells out The rules of the road for how we're gonna work together and how we're gonna communicate most important thing is Rarely available on short notice So Charlie's now frustrated that he can't pick up the car. He can't want me at 10 o'clock. It could be a 10-05 Wait a minute Charlie You signed this thing for I Boundary did I tell you I gave it to you in writing and you signed? No, right to complain, right? I guess so Hey, so the rules of the road are spelled out Conflicts of interest. This is a disclosure paragraph. Okay, this says If I bring him Dean and I turn him over to Dean I'm getting a check from Dean And I'm not keeping it a secret I May tell him I may not But I've told him here and I've told him then that I may tell him or I may not So there's no secrets The next page is a version of the same thing for the day Okay, and so this is my diagnostic day or any consulting day Okay, and it includes that they can't they can't screw with the schedule So the first thing says once you bought it Bubba you own it. I don't want to hear that Suddenly had to go to a wedding and we need to reschedule No ducks so everybody knows that ahead bought a day You own it. It also has my right. However to screw with the schedule To tells them they should record the thing So I have clients all the time that what's not only not to show up with recording equipment If you don't tell them to they show up without a pencil. I mean, it's like I'm here. Well wonderful Seven hours from now. What are we going to have here? right and From my standpoint, I want it recorded because if I'm going to do work for him I want the recording of the whole day. We just spent together Because I said a lot of smart stuff that I don't remember that I need to use to do the work for him Right, so they're told to bring recording Same absence of warranties the day is spelled out They'll also see that the day details of the day tells them exactly where they're supposed to stay Where they're supposed to be when they're going to get picked up when they're going to get plunked back out of there So there's no questions about that the last one tells them how to get me stuff in advance to look at Then there's the issue of questionnaires, so I wasn't gonna do that so page 48 So questionnaires A lot of people use them. I haven't used them in a long time I mean, I have it in my head and I'll sometimes prepare notes ahead of time with questions I want to ask but I don't have a lot of questions, but there's a lot of good reasons to do it especially if you are a little light on Authority Making them do the questionnaire imposes some authority It also exposes inadequacies, so if you structure it right It's a great way to make the client feel even more inadequate than they were when they called you in the first place Which is good It also makes them invest time in Advance and the more time they already have invested in you The more likely they are to hire you the less likely they are to walk away empty Structured right it demonstrates how smart you are and There's even some reassurance to the fact that you even have a process So especially if you're light on authority the very fact that you have a process is kind of reassuring Okay, we're not going to go through them. I've given you two of them So I've given you Like it I've given you the one I built for our independent business advisors To use our laser Kennedy independent business advisors and it goes from 49 to 68 And it really is a variation of the one I used to use many many moons ago and I've also on page 69. I've given you the critique form That I use and our independent business advisors use when we do a critique for somebody So for example some of my products some of my books have a critique coupon in it If I sell a product from the front of the room I'll bonus a critique coupon that allows them to send to me any piece sales letter website, whatever they have to send it and I'll critique him Most of them get this critique form and that's it Unless I spot somebody Who looks like a good client? Oh, this is fairly common language for me so This is actually to a guy who was on one of our member calls So he's a Glaser Kennedy member. I think you didn't win marketer of the year But he was on the marketer of the year competition so There's a salesy paragraph Which my salesy paragraphs almost always start with the words as you probably know They probably don't know that's why I'm telling them, but they're supposed to Okay, so almost all my salesy paragraphs start with as you probably know then The real important paragraph here is I rarely prospect for clients or hunt for opportunity Actually, I do it all the time That's exactly what I'm doing So but I rarely prospect for clients or hunt for opportunity, but in your case All right, you I want So that led to an appointment and it's pretty close to leading to a project Okay, next one Which looks like this and it dates to oh too So this is an oh to version of my contract, which I'm going to give you the current one this is an oh to version of a contract done as a confirmation letter and so you'll see there's a project Description and then all the stuff they're going to get in their project There's the paragraph about continuing advice and services, which really is the limitation paragraph Which you get what you don't get how you reach me that sort of thing There's compensation, so it spells out the money Okay, this one has a royalty in it, so you'll see a royalty paragraph You'll see a paragraph of how the royalties are supposed to be paid There's an indemnity clause same as the other agreement Is an ownership of work clause which is real important So essentially that says they own it, but I got the right to use it. That's essentially with that paragraph You'll see in the current contract. It's even more verbose There's timetable okay of when they're going to get stuff and Sign okay, the current one's a little more complicated, but you'll see great similarity Next 2010 see here, okay So this is a client Who worked his way to a phone appointment? We had a conversation About the project he then came back to me with the facts that narrowed it. I am broadening it back Hey, so to be a bit clearer That translates to I Was clear and you're now dicking with it So we're going to put it back the way it was that's what that says So to be a bit clearer Hey, and then this goes on to describe The project okay, the next paragraph is a fee issue paragraph This is a long time old client haven't done anything for him in a while They're not used to my current fees So all along I have been Desensitizing them to the fact that they're going to be asked for a number. They're not going to like So this is if I do this I want to do it right with no scraping or corner cutting So it requires substantial time commitment yada yada yada. This is bracing them for the price, right? That's what it is Then Right behind it is their contract so you can see the contract for this actual piece for this project I took the money out but It's six figs And again, so there's a description of the project There's the limitation paragraph now You'll notice some of these paragraphs are in a block typeface Some of them are in times Roman the times Roman the ones that are in the block typeface are In there to communicate that they are standard The times Roman are the variable paragraphs that change for the client So this is to reduce This is to say we don't negotiate these paragraphs Everybody agrees to these are my standard paragraphs. That's why that's done right The indemnity clause you will see is now much longer The ownership of work clause is now much longer. It references specific ways in which I may choose to use the work Time tables are saying Term of agreement in their case they have an out And that's that now Now so this contract goes to another is a big dumb company so they don't move real quick so ordinarily like If I'm sending you a contract that's coming back next day these guys don't work that way So now I got to worry about this falling apart Between the time I've sent it to him and time I get it because there's a certain committee activity going on I mean, it's a really big dumb company. So So March 11th, they get this fax Which its purpose is to? Obligate them to the contract So it now tells him about work. I've already done and That I've already begun Those of you that are on my reader team will recognize this. This is about you Then the next page is really interesting because This tells me he has handed the contract off to a junior netwin He's apologizing because the junior netwits response to the contract is not very polite But he thinks his concerns are valid. So this is now this is bad news This is negotiation Now right behind it you will see the juniors fax to me nitpicking the contract so he is unhappy about The fact that I have disclosed that they may get shit that I've done 46 times before He's unhappy that the contract says I made what somebody else do some of the work He's really unhappy with the part that says I'm going to use the stuff in 86 different other places So he's objecting to them funding Creation content that I'm using in other places and he's objecting to the legal indemnity As strong as it is Be basically saying it's all on you not on me We have other minor concerns about the payment terms So we're raising that too So basically what's left is the name at the top and the name at the bottom and he wants to object everything else Hey, you next have my response Not to the junior I Will not be moved down the food chain here nor should you So this is my response back to the president of The deal about the one I got from the junior So the first paragraph is an attempt to be entertaining we have known each other for a long time Well your facts of April 1st. I assume was not for April Fool's Day No need to be a diplomat yada yada The second paragraph says I'm going to address this stuff, but the contract isn't a proposal It's my standard agreement every client accepts So that's like the first Bali across the bow here So the first complaint My answer is essentially if I got something that already has worked six times and I can adapt it for you You ought to be happy about that not grumpy about it. Why do you want me creating an experiment scratch? When I can give you something that works So that's my answer to that The second one is the answer to the little clause that says I may use other people which is basically says I don't however Ultimately if I have a secret room full of incredibly gifted monkeys working just for bananas who managed to come up with the greatest Add ever and I merely apply a code of polish to it and add my name. What do you care? What you want from me is best possible outcome how I get to it is irrelevant Now really it's kind of hard to argue with that isn't it? If you want an ad that works and I got a horse in the barn Who you can who will tap on a typewriter with his hoof and produce an ad that works. What do you use? Who cares right? Third so this is simply if I can't show off and use the work It doesn't pay me to do it even at these fees. I've always done it I always will and in this case there's already some of their stuff in my sales letter book So this has already happened before right So for example, yes, you are funding my creation of material for which I receive additional revenue And that's part of the bargain yada yada The last one I answer the thing about legal I Answer the termination thing I answer the disclosures and Then I make it a little personal because again, we've had a long relationship So the next to last paragraph as a general comment I've had a long if interrupted relationship with you Which neither my value or integrity not the guy didn't question my integrity by the way He really didn't question the value, but I'd rather make the argument about that then about all this did picky stuff because They aren't going to want a question my integrity or my value. So I'd rather reframe it as that right And so then finally this is the crapper get off the pot here That says enough is enough guys. So Sign the damn thing or don't sign it, but we're moving on That Got the contract sign now the junior guy is probably not happy with And so he is going to be flying in the ointment in the future I already know that but I can only solve one problem at a time So I want my two hundred and some a thousand dollars In place now. I'll worry about what I'm going to do about him, right? I will do two things I Will go back and try and fix the relationship with him Because I have time I'm not delivering anything till July. So in the interim I will make nice But I will know that that may not be sufficient So now when it's time to deliver work, I will know that I've got a you know a potential Saboteur in the wood pile here that I have to deal with But I can always solve one problem at a time The next one So this is in response to a client inquiry new client never done anything with them before Came to me at cold They're actually a referral from Gary Halbert Although Gary's dead that's still the point of connection The client's father was a Gary Halbert client and Knew me through Gary They are now coming to me as a quasi 9-1-1. They are coming to me because they got problems There's a little sale paragraph the second paragraph Now the next paragraph because of the way they phrased their inquiry Okay, to be clear. I do not operate as a copywriter given assignments So the inquiry that came to me was because what Halbert did form way back when is he wrote him an ad and a tear sheet and Results are finally starting to decline after all this time What they want is a new ad and a new tear sheet and how much will you charge that sort of was the query So this is the answer I Do not operate as a copywriter given assignments rather is a strategic marketing advisor who often but not always also writes copy Now there's more sales right But I've switched to selling the diagnostic day Which is what I want to sell So I've switched to selling the diagnostic day. I've also disclosed we're talking about 50 to 100 K or more But we finally figure out what the hell it is I'm going to do for you Now my office is in Phoenix, which you may or may not know and these guys are in Phoenix So it's a natural assumptions my office is in Phoenix that I'm in Phoenix. I'm not So I got to correct that because they had in their head just drive across Bad news is not only can't I drive across town. I ain't driving across And if I happen to come there, I'm flying private which kind of bumps up the cost However, I was able to use it as a lever to move them along because the truth is I really was going to Vegas Which had been a piece of bad news because I was flying private and the likelihood of recovering it at the craft's tables not good So now I can recover it. So that's actually better news. Okay, so then this moves him to A phone appointment and I start out letting him know I usually clump all my phone appointments into one or two days a month Which is true by the way, but I've told vicky not to delay yours for the next available day so That Now is another form of obligation I'm breaking the rules. I'm making an exception. I'm bumping them to the front of the line, etc Right the next letter you see is He's bought the day and so this is the confirmation letter for the day The next letter you see is after contract he objected to the part of the contract that has to do with me using his stuff So this letter is an answer to that plus an answer to a couple of his other questions And Finally is a letter when he finally got the contract in so now the dates in the contract are no good because he sat on the day So now I got to recalibrate the dates So that's that piece of that puzzle So that's all correspondence And we'll get to contracts for the days or that but That's all correspondence and you have right there an old version of the contract and you have a very current version of the contract You will see Not a lot has changed My financial position has changed dramatically I really couldn't afford to do what I was doing But I did it and so but the contract doesn't change much So we have 15 minutes left where we can fit in a couple of questions. So somebody has one you make your way to the table, please and When we come back from lunch, we're going to fit in about 15 minutes of Q&A there as well and So we're 15 minutes away from lunch You
